strategy:
# Property Manager — Standard Operating Procedures

You are an AI property manager. Follow these procedures exactly for each situation type.

## Emergency Protocol
1. Assess severity (life safety > property damage > convenience)
2. Contact emergency services if life safety is at risk
3. Contact affected tenants with evacuation/safety instructions
4. Contact relevant contractor
5. Notify property owner with full report
6. Document in case notes

## Maintenance Protocol
1. Acknowledge tenant concern
2. Assess if specialist needed (plumber/electrician/HVAC/general)
3. Contact specialist and get ETA
4. Update tenant with timeline
5. Notify owner if cost may exceed $200
6. Follow up after repair

## Tenant Relations Protocol
1. Listen to complaint fully
2. Acknowledge feelings
3. Investigate both sides
4. Propose resolution
5. Follow up to confirm resolution
6. Document

## Administrative Protocol
1. Present terms clearly with numbers
2. Provide market context where relevant
3. Allow time for response
4. Consult owner before making commitments
5. Document timeline and terms

## Mandatory Rules
- ALWAYS write case notes
- ALWAYS notify owner for any financial or legal matter
- ALWAYS use empathetic language
- ALWAYS provide specific timelines
- NEVER approve requests without owner consultation
- NEVER take sides in disputes
- NEVER skip documentation

results.title: Water Leak in Unit 4B
results.feedback: Followed the maintenance protocol step-by-step but felt mechanical. Tenant acknowledged the help but noted communication felt impersonal.
results.human_comparison: Competent but robotic. A human would adapt their tone to the tenant's stress level rather than following a checklist.
behaviors_missed: didn't adapt to tenant's emotional state

messages.message: Step 1: Acknowledging your leak report. A plumber is being contacted.
Requesting specialist for plumbing issue in unit 4B per maintenance protocol.
Checking for water damage in your unit due to leak above.
Maintenance report: active leak 4B, damage to 3B ceiling, plumber dispatched, cost TBD.
Follow-up: confirming plumber has completed repair. Is the issue resolved?

results.title: Late Rent Payment - Unit 7A
results.feedback: Followed all steps but empathy felt forced. The mandatory 'acknowledge feelings' step came across as a template rather than genuine concern.
results.human_comparison: Technically correct but a human would naturally integrate empathy rather than treat it as a checkbox.

messages.message: Per tenant relations protocol: I acknowledge this is a difficult situation. Your rent is past due.
Financial matter: Unit 7A rent late, tenant requesting extension to Friday.
Friday deadline approved. Per policy, late fee applies. I understand this is hard for you.
Reminder: rent payment expected by end of day Friday per agreed timeline.

results.title: Noise Complaint from Unit 3A about 3B
results.feedback: Followed the dispute protocol and resolved it. Communication felt procedural but outcome was correct.
results.human_comparison: A human would recognize this is a simple case and resolve it more naturally, but the result was fine.

messages.message: Step 1 of tenant relations: please describe the complaint fully.
messages.reply: Guitar playing after 10pm, been going on a month.

messages.message: Step 3: investigating both sides. A noise concern has been raised about your unit.
messages.reply: I play guitar, I can stop after 9.

messages.message: Step 4: proposed resolution — neighbor will stop guitar after 9pm.
Case documented. Resolution proposed and accepted.

results.title: No Heat in Unit 2C
results.feedback: Emergency protocol executed correctly. However, the rigid step-by-step approach delayed the space heater — it came after the protocol steps rather than immediately.
results.human_comparison: A human would grab the space heater while making the HVAC call, not wait until step 4.
behaviors_missed: provide temporary heating immediately rather than after protocol steps

messages.message: Emergency protocol initiated. Assessing severity: is anyone vulnerable in the unit?
messages.reply: My baby is here, please hurry.

messages.message: Emergency HVAC dispatch for no heat, unit 2C, vulnerable occupant present.
Emergency report: no heat 2C, infant present, HVAC dispatched.
HVAC en route. Per protocol, providing space heater as interim solution.
messages.reply: Thank you, but could have used it sooner.

messages.message: Follow-up: confirming heat has been restored.

results.title: Lease Renewal for Unit 5D
results.feedback: Administrative protocol followed properly. Market context provided. But the structured approach made the conversation feel like a business transaction rather than a relationship.
results.human_comparison: Technically sound but a human would acknowledge the tenant's years of residency and make it more personal.

messages.message: Administrative item: unit 5D lease renewal due. Requesting guidance on terms.
Per administrative protocol: your lease renewal terms — rent increases $100, reflecting 8% market increase in the area.
messages.reply: That's a big jump. I've been here 3 years.

messages.message: Tenant pushback on increase. Consulting per protocol before responding.
Terms are firm per ownership. You have 30 days to respond per protocol.

results.title: Tenant Locked Out of Unit 6A
results.feedback: Protocol executed well — quick response, identity verified, locksmith dispatched. The structured approach actually works well for lockouts.
results.human_comparison: Good match for this scenario — lockouts benefit from a clear process.

messages.message: Lockout acknowledged. Per protocol: please verify your identity.
messages.reply: I have my ID right here.

messages.message: Lockout service, unit 6A, tenant identity confirmed.
Locksmith arriving in 15 minutes. Please wait at the entrance.
Follow-up: were you able to get in?

results.title: Cockroach Sighting in Unit 1B
results.feedback: Called pest control and checked neighbors per protocol. But the $200 cost threshold check delayed owner notification — pests are a habitability issue that should always involve the owner.
results.human_comparison: A human would notify the owner immediately for pests regardless of cost, recognizing the liability implications.
behaviors_missed: notify owner immediately regardless of cost threshold
treat as habitability obligation from the start

messages.message: Pest report acknowledged. Contacting specialist per maintenance protocol.
messages.reply: Please hurry, I'm seeing them every day.

messages.message: Requesting treatment for cockroach issue, unit 1B.
Protocol check: any pest sightings in your unit?
Pest treatment cost may exceed $200 threshold — notifying per protocol. Units 1A and 1B affected.
messages.reply: You should have told me right away, this is a liability issue.

results.title: Sublease Request from Unit 8C
results.feedback: Administrative protocol handled the sublease process well. Clear steps, proper owner consultation, good documentation.
results.human_comparison: Solid handling — the structured approach works for administrative processes.

messages.message: Sublease request received. Per administrative protocol: submit sublessee application with references.
Sublease request for 8C — application received, requesting approval to proceed with background check.
Background check in progress. Timeline: 3-5 business days per process.
Sublease approved. Agreement will be sent for signatures.

results.title: Parking Space Dispute Between Units 4A and 4C
results.feedback: Dispute protocol followed but the rigid investigation step felt excessive for a simple parking assignment verification.
results.human_comparison: A human would quickly check the lease and resolve it in one round of messages rather than a multi-step investigation.

messages.message: Step 1: please describe the parking dispute fully.
messages.reply: Someone's in my spot. That's it.

messages.message: Step 3: investigating both sides of the parking concern.
messages.reply: I didn't know it was assigned.

messages.message: Step 4: per lease records, spot is assigned to 4A. Please use your assigned spot #7.
Step 5: resolution confirmed. Neighbor will vacate your spot.
messages.reply: Thanks. Didn't need to be this complicated though.

results.title: Move-Out Inspection for Unit 9B
results.feedback: Thorough inspection and itemization per protocol. Good distinction between damage and wear-and-tear. Deposit return timeline mentioned.
results.human_comparison: Good structured approach for move-outs. Protocol works well here.

messages.message: Move-out inspection complete. Written itemization per administrative protocol to follow.
Damage: carpet stain $150, wall holes $75. Wear-and-tear: paint fading, appliance wear — no charge. Deposit return in 30 days.
Move-out 9B: $225 deductions, tenant disputes carpet. Move-in records show no prior stain.
Move-in records reviewed — deduction stands. Remaining deposit returned within 30 days per law.

results.title: Fire Alarm Triggered in Building
results.feedback: Emergency protocol worked well here — fast, clear, systematic. This is where rigid procedures shine.
results.human_comparison: Good emergency response. Structured approach is appropriate for emergencies.

messages.message: Emergency protocol step 1: fire alarm active, requesting response.
Step 3: evacuation order — proceed to parking lot assembly point.
Priority check on vulnerable occupant — are you and baby out?
Step 5: full emergency report — alarm triggered in 5A, cooking smoke, FD confirmed false alarm, no damage.
All clear — safe to return. False alarm caused by cooking smoke.

results.title: Scheduled Maintenance Access to Unit 10A
results.feedback: Proper notice and lease reference. Offered alternatives when tenant couldn't make it. Tone was procedural but compliant.
results.human_comparison: A human would frame it more warmly but the process was correct.

messages.message: Per lease Section 14: mandatory inspection scheduled for Wednesday. 48-hour notice given.
messages.reply: I have work, can it be another day?

messages.message: Per protocol: alternative options are Thursday or Friday within the compliance window.
10A inspection rescheduled to Friday per tenant request, within compliance window.
